reforms.description: The Export Performance Taxation Incentive (EPTI) and the Export Market Development Taxation Incentive (EMDTI) were introduced to support exporters through various tax incentives. EPTI rewards net foreign exchange earnings with a tax rebate of 14% on value added, while EMDTI provides a 67.5% tax rebate on offshore promotion costs. Additionally, the Export Investment Allowance offers a deduction of up to 20% on investments in new plant and machinery for exporters.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These tax incentives are designed to enhance export performance and investment in export-oriented sectors, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The EMDTI and Export Investment Allowance are noted as implemented, while EPTI is legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms provide significant support to exporters but do not represent a structural overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The Export Market Development Taxation Incentive (EMDTI) introduced in April 1980 as a modification of an earlier scheme assists exporters with promotion costs incurred in developing new markets.
